plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the complete plan for adding Top-A sampling to vLLM.

## Summary

**Algorithm**: `threshold = top_a Ă— max(probs)` â€” tokens with probability below the threshold are masked to `-inf`. At `top_a=0.0` (default) nothing is filtered. At `top_a=1.0` only the highest-probability token(s) survive. This is mathematically identical to `min_p` filtering, so the implementation pattern is a direct copy-and-adapt.

## 9 files to change

| # | File | Change |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Add `top_a: float = 0.0` field, `[0,1]` validation, greedy-mode reset to `0.0`, `from_optional()` param, `__repr__` entry |
| 2 | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` to `SamplingTensors`; collect per-request values in the loop; build pinned-memory tensor; add `do_top_a` bool to the return tuple (extends 4-tuple â†’ 5-tuple) |
| 3 | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | Unpack 5-tuple in `_init_sampling_tensors`; add `_apply_top_a()` (mirrors `_apply_min_p`); call it in `forward()` after `do_min_p` block |
| 4 | `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | Add `top_a: Optional[float] = None`, default `0.0`, extraction, and pass-through in all three request classes (`ChatCompletionRequest`, `CompletionRequest`, `TranscriptionRequest`) |
| 5 | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | Add `TopALogitsProcessor` (exact clone of `MinPLogitsProcessor` with `top_a` substitution); register it in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` under `argmax_invariant` |
| 6 | `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py` | Add `or sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS` to `is_spec_decode_unsupported()` |
| 7 | `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` | Mirror every `min_p` tensor/set bookkeeping for `top_a` (init, add_request, remove_request, swap, condense, property) |
| 8 | `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | Add `top_a` to defaults dict, dataclass field, fill_slice + copy in `from_input_batch()` |
| 9 | `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` | Add `apply_top_a()` method; call after `apply_min_p` in `sample()` |
